metadata.summary: PMP exam weights shift July 2026: People 42%→33%, Process 50%→41%, Business Environment 8%→26%. Study plans must front-load Business Environment topics.

content.title: PM Certification Exam Changes 2026
content.summary: The PMP exam form active from July 1, 2026 shifts domain weights significantly: People drops from 42% to 33%, Process from 50% to 41%, and Business Environment rises from 8% to 26% (+18pp). Business Environment now covers strategic alignment, governance, sustainability, value delivery measurement, and AI in project management. Study plans must front-load Business Environment, which most candidates have historically under-prepared.

why.p: LLMs trained before mid-2025 emit the old weights (42/50/8) confidently and frame PMP content around "5 process groups + 10 knowledge areas" from PMBoK 6. A candidate using pre-2026 materials without explicit weight correction will systematically under-prepare Business Environment and fail the new exam distribution. The shift correlates with PMBoK 8 emphasis on value delivery, governance, and sustainability.

The methodology readme is a stub (~45 lines) — there is no example bank, no question-style guidance, no tailoring case studies. Treat it as a pointer, not a study plan. The +18% Business Environment shift covers a heterogeneous set of topics (sustainability, governance, AI, value delivery) — agents over-index on whichever is in their training data.

list.item: Helping a PMP candidate plan a study schedule targeting the new domain weights.
Auditing existing PMP/CAPM training content for Business Environment topic gaps.
Re-tagging an in-house PM knowledge base to match the post-July-2026 weighting.
Building a practice question sampler that respects the new 33/41/26 distribution.
Comparing PMP vs Disciplined Agile vs PRINCE2 2025 cert paths.
General project execution work — exam alignment is academic, not operational; use performance-domains-overview instead.
Candidates sitting before July 1, 2026 — they take the old form; new weights are misleading.
Non-PMI certifications (PRINCE2, IPMA, AgilePM) — the weight shift is PMI-specific.

section.name: weight-changes
section.p: Domain weight shifts effective July 1 2026, with new emphasis areas and study allocation formula.

list.item: People: 42% → 33% (down 9pp) — leadership, team development, conflict, collaboration
Process: 50% → 41% (down 9pp) — scheduling, cost, risk, procurement, quality
Business Environment: 8% → 26% (up 18pp) — strategy, governance, sustainability, value delivery, AI in PM

section.name: new-business-environment-topics
section.p: The Business Environment domain now covers strategic alignment, governance, sustainability, and emerging topics like AI in project management. These topics were previously scattered or absent from earlier exams.

list.item: Business case development and benefits realisation
Strategic alignment and portfolio thinking
Governance structures and compliance
Sustainability in projects (GPM P5, ISO 21502)
Value delivery measurement (outcomes, not outputs)
Tailoring approaches across delivery methods
AI and technology in project management (new testable topic)

section.name: study-allocation-formula
section.p: Allocate study hours proportional to (weight x gap), not weight alone. A candidate already strong in People should allocate less time there even though People is 33% of the exam.

list.item: hours_domain = total_study_hours x (weight x (1 - mastery_score))
Normalise across domains so hours sum to total_study_hours
Budget at least 30% of practice items on Business Environment even if weight is 26% — candidates historically underweight new sections

section.p: The PMI Exam Content Outline (ECO) is the authoritative source. Always cite the ECO version date (Q3 2025 outline → July 2026 exam) in study plans and content audits. Do not rely on cached training data for ECO task lists.

rule.statement: Force-feed the 2026 domain weights (33/41/26) in every system prompt that generates PMP study content; never let the agent infer weights from training data.
rule.rationale: LLMs trained before mid-2025 emit the old weights (42/50/8) confidently; explicit injection is the only reliable fix.

rule.statement: Restrict "Business Environment" to PMI vocabulary only; reject AI-generated content that conflates it with SOC, ITIL, or general business strategy terms.
rule.rationale: Business Environment is an ITIL and SOC term with different meanings; cross-pollination produces incorrect exam answers.

rule.statement: Fetch the current PMI ECO PDF before generating any study plan; do not use cached or inferred task lists.
rule.rationale: ECO layout and task lists change each cycle; stale ECO data produces study plans misaligned with the actual exam.

rule.statement: Cite one sustainability reference per generated answer (GPM P5, ISO 21502, or ISO 14001); do not mix standards within a single answer.
rule.rationale: PMI cites multiple sustainability standards with different scopes; mixing produces inconsistent and potentially incorrect guidance.

rule.statement: Distinguish AI as exam content (what candidates must know about AI in PM) from AI used in studying (what tools candidates use); never conflate the two.
rule.rationale: AI-in-PM is now a testable topic; conflation confuses exam preparation with tool usage.

antipattern.description: Generating practice questions using "processes" vs "principles" wording when they should reference 2026 ECO task language.
antipattern.reason: Old wording anchors candidates in PMBoK 6 framing; 2026 questions use PMBoK 7/8 outcome-based language.

antipattern.description: Practice question banks generated by agents that drift toward old domain percentages when sampling.
antipattern.reason: Without explicit weight constraints, sampling produces 42/50/8 distribution by default; enforce 33/41/26 in the sampler.

antipattern.description: Using third-party exam prep content without verifying the publication date aligns with the 2026 ECO update.
antipattern.reason: Pre-2025 content reflects old weights and will mislead candidates for the new exam form.

section.name: agentic-workflow
section.p: The agent is a study-plan generator and gap analyst, not a tutor. Inputs: candidate's exam date, current knowledge state (self-assessment per domain), available study capacity per week. Output: a weighted study plan that allocates time proportional to new domain weights, plus a gap report flagging Business Environment topics where coverage is thin. A second agent samples practice questions from a verified question bank with the new weight distribution. Humans verify cert-board accuracy via the official PMI ECO before trusting numbers.

list.item: eco-fetcher — fetches the PMI Exam Content Outline PDF, parses tasks per domain, emits structured JSON.
study-planner — given candidate state + ECO, outputs a weekly plan honouring the 33/41/26 split.
gap-analyst — compares a knowledge base against the ECO, reports uncovered tasks.
question-sampler — samples N items from a question bank with the new weight distribution, balancing People/Process/BE.

section.name: cli-tools-and-services
section.p: Tools for parsing ECO PDFs, generating study decks, and tracking progress:

list.item: pdfplumber / pypdf — Parse the PMI ECO PDF into structured tasks
gh / git — Version-control study plans + gap analyses
pandoc — Convert study plan markdown → Anki / PDF
genanki (Python) — Generate Anki decks weighted to new exam
obsidian-cli — Sync notes into Obsidian vault for spaced repetition

section.p: Services and apps for study and practice:

list.item: PMI Authorized Training Partner content — Official source; mostly proprietary
PrepCast / PMTraining — Question banks; check post-2026 update date
Pocket Prep / PMP Exam Prep — Question quality varies; verify weights
Anki / RemNote — Spaced repetition, scriptable (OSS / Freemium)
Notion / Obsidian — Study notebook (SaaS / OSS, file/REST friendly)
Coursera / LinkedIn Learning — Course content; check publish date
GoalQuest / Mometrix — Practice tests
Disciplined Agile Browser (PMI) — DA decision tables, free with PMI membership
Pin the ECO version date in your study plan (Q3 2025 outline → July 2026 exam). Do not let agents conflate ECO versions.
Allocate study time proportional to (weight × gap), not weight alone. A candidate strong in People should still spend less time there.
Budget 30%+ of practice items on Business Environment despite its 26% weight; many candidates underweight the new section.
Read the PMI Exam Reference List (the published source list); skip third-party content that does not cite it.
The shift to Business Environment correlates with case-study-style scenario questions; practice scenario interpretation, not flashcards.
Track sustainability + AI-in-PM topics separately — they are easy to miss inside the broad Business Environment label.
LLMs trained before mid-2025 emit old weights (42/50/8) confidently; force-feed the new split in the system prompt.
"Business Environment" is also a SOC term and an ITIL term; LLMs cross-pollinate concepts — restrict to PMI vocabulary.
Sustainability content varies (GPM-P5 vs ISO 21502 vs PRiSM); pin one reference per generated answer.
ECO PDFs change layout each cycle; brittle PDF parsers break — re-validate parsing with a known-good fixture.
Practice questions generated by agents drift toward old wording ("processes" vs "principles"); ground them in the 2026 ECO sample items.
The methodology readme is itself thin; agents will hallucinate detail — require external citations or refuse.
AI-in-PM is now a tested topic; agents must distinguish AI as exam content vs AI used in studying.

template.purpose: Weekly study plan template with domain-weighted hour allocation per domain by weight x gap
